originalTitle: Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
title: Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue

originalTitle: Ugandan opposition leader goes abroad after two months in hiding
title: Ugandan opposition leader goes abroad after two months in hiding
content: Uganda's military chief has said Bobi Wine is wanted "dead or alive", although some ministers have denied he is being sought.

originalTitle: Will Trump help or hinder Zimbabwe's white farmers in their compensation battle?
title: Farmers in Zimbabwe Ask for a Helping Hand!
content:
Hello, little friends! Let's talk about some special people called farmers in a beautiful country in Africa called Zimbabwe. They need a little bit of help, and we're going to learn why in a simple story.

Meet the Farmers!

Farmers are super important people. They plant tiny seeds and grow yummy food for all of us to eat every day. Did you know? Farmers in Zimbabwe grow maize (which is like corn), vegetables, and delicious fruits like mangoes and bananas! Imagine a big field full of happy plants, like juicy tomatoes or tall corn stalks. That's a farm! Farmers work very hard under the warm sun and sometimes in the rain to make sure we have food on our plates to keep us strong and healthy.

What Happened to Their Land?

A long, long time ago, many farmers in Zimbabwe had big farms. They spent their lives growing lots of food on these lands. But then, some important things changed in Zimbabwe. The country decided that the land needed to be shared differently among all its people. This meant that many farmers, who were mostly white, had to leave their farms. They didn't have their farms anymore to grow food and live there. It was a big change, and it made many farmers feel sad because farming was their whole life and how they took care of their families.

What is "Compensation"?

When you lose something important to you, like your favourite toy, you might feel sad. Sometimes, someone might give you something new or some money to make you feel better and fair. This is called "compensation." It means getting something fair back for what you lost. It's like saying sorry with a helpful gift. The farmers who lost their land in Zimbabwe want compensation. They want to be paid fairly for the farms, the homes, and all the hard work they put into the land that they had to leave behind. They believe it's important to be fair to everyone, even when big changes happen in a country. Everyone deserves fairness.

Asking for Help: A Special Firm

So, these farmers decided they needed to ask for help. They found a special group of people called a "lobbying firm." Did you know? Zimbabwe is a country in Southern Africa! It has amazing animals like elephants, lions, giraffes, and rhinos! Imagine you want to talk to a very important person, like your school principal, to ask for something important. Sometimes, it's easier and better if a trusted grown-up helps you talk to them. A lobbying firm is like that grown-up helper. They are very good at talking to important leaders and telling them about the farmers' story. They ask for help on behalf of the farmers. This firm is very good at talking to big leaders and making sure their message about fairness and compensation is heard clearly and kindly.

Talking to the US President!

Guess who this special lobbying firm is trying to talk to? The President of a big country far, far away called America! The President of America is a very important leader. His name is Mr. Trump. He makes big decisions for his country. The farmers hope that Mr. Trump, the big leader from America, might be able to help them get their compensation. Why would Mr. Trump help? Because America is a very powerful country, and its leaders often talk to leaders in other countries to help solve problems or ensure fairness. Maybe Mr. Trump can talk to the leaders in Zimbabwe and ask them to help the farmers get their fair compensation. He can be a helper from far away. It's like when your parents talk to your friend's parents to help you both solve a problem. Important people can help other important people talk and understand each other.

Will It Work?

This is a big question! No one knows for sure if Mr. Trump will help or exactly how much he can help the farmers. Did you know? The capital city of Zimbabwe is called Harare! It's a busy and beautiful city. But the farmers are very hopeful. They are trying their best to get their message out to the world and get the fairness they believe they deserve. It's like planting a tiny seed and hoping it grows into a strong, beautiful plant. They are planting the seed of their request and hoping it grows into real help for them.

Why This Story Matters to Us

This story might seem far away, but it teaches us many important things that are close to our hearts. It teaches us about fairness – that everyone, no matter where they are from, should be treated fairly and with kindness. It teaches us about helping others – that sometimes, people need to ask for help, and it's good when important people listen and try to make things right. And it reminds us that farmers everywhere are super important! They grow the food that keeps us healthy and strong every single day. So, let's wish these farmers in Zimbabwe good luck as they try to get their helping hand and find a fair solution! Thank you for listening, little friends!
summary: Farmers in Zimbabwe, who lost their land, are asking a special group to talk to the US President, Mr. Trump, to help them get paid fairly for what they lost.
whyMatters: This is important because it's about being fair to everyone, helping people when they need it, and remembering how important farmers are for growing our food and keeping us healthy.

originalTitle: Strategic oil release may calm markets but cannot fix Hormuz disruption
title: Strategic oil release may calm markets but cannot fix Hormuz disruption
content: Record oil release steadies markets, but war-driven disruption in the Strait of Hormuz keeps energy supplies uncertain.

title: We've got a special player on our hands with Dowman - Hart
